_doc: The quality-tools registry — the one home for 'what does GREEN mean in this project' (PROTOCOL.md `## Quality tools`). This is the TEMPLATE: it ships only the SHAPE plus one example row. A real project REPLACES the example with its own stack's tools (chosen at `/dev-setup`) and drops each tool's native config beside this file (eslint.config.mjs, ruff.toml, semgrep.yml, …). The Builder runs every `build`-beat tool and hands back only when they pass; the Reviewer confirms the `review`-beat tools ran and reads their output. The runner is `node src/quality/run.mjs <beat>`. No core edit is ever needed to add or change a tool — only a row here.

_row_shape.checks: one plain line: what this tool catches
_row_shape.config: path to its native config beside this file, or null if none
_row_shape.run: the command to run it (from the project root)
_row_shape.beat: build | review | ship — which loop beat it runs in
_row_shape.init: one line: how to install/enable it (the minimal init instruction)

tools.checks: EXAMPLE ROW — replace with your stack's linter (eslint / ruff / …). A red tool is not green.
tools.run: echo 'replace this example row with a real quality tool for your stack'
tools.init: see PROTOCOL.md `## Quality tools` — add a row per tool, drop its config beside this file
